reforms.description: The Austrian government implemented several investment promotion schemes aimed at stimulating investment in small businesses and industry. These include a total increase of Sch. 230 million in subsidies, interest subsidies that reduce credit costs by 3 percentage points below the market interest rate, and a scheme encouraging private investment in productive sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investment promotion schemes are designed to enhance business investment, which is expected to improve productivity and economic competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has already implemented these investment promotion schemes.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant but not structural changes to the investment landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Increase in existing investment promotion schemes for small scale business by a total of Sch. 230 million, comprising additional subsidies for investment in the tourism sector (+ Sch. 120 million), small crafts (+ Sch. 52 million), and other small business (+Sch. 58 million).

reforms.description: The Federal budget for 1979 includes a comprehensive package of measures aimed at promoting investment, focusing on projects that enhance the international competitiveness of the Austrian economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The measures are intended to boost investment in competitive sectors, which should lead to improved economic performance.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures are included in the Federal budget for 1979.
reforms.importance_rationale: This represents a major reform aimed at enhancing the competitiveness of the economy through targeted investment.
reforms.source_quote: The total amount of both expenditure reductions and revenue increases included in this latest package is Sch. 6 billion.
